summary: AnySpot Phase 2 (Partner dashboard + waitlist) end-to-end testing. Backend: 8/8 new pytest cases pass covering /api/partner/studios (4 studios), /api/partner/overview (full KPI/top_classes/upcoming_roster shape), /api/partner/classes (upcoming filter), POST/PATCH/DELETE partner classes, waitlist create (no credit deduction, waitlist_count increment, duplicate-block returns 400 'Already booked', waitlist cancel only decrements waitlist_count), and auto-promote-on-confirmed-cancel (verified via synthetic 2nd user in Mongo: promoted user's status -> confirmed, credits deducted, spots_left=0, waitlist_count=0). Phase 1's 12 tests still pass (no regression). Frontend: /partner renders partner-dashboard, 4 KPI cards, upcoming-roster panel with fill bars, top-classes, classes-table with edit + delete. Add Class dialog opens, all form fields work (studio select 4 options, category select 5 options, title/instructor/start/duration/credits/capacity/description). Saving creates the class, toast 'Class created' shown, table refreshes. Edit dialog prefills correctly and persists changes. Delete shows confirm-delete-dialog, on confirm class is removed and toast 'Class deleted. Attendees refunded.' appears. view-roster-{id} opens roster-dialog. Waitlist UI verified: a freshly created capacity=1 class shows 'Book' on Explore; after booking, button text becomes 'Join waitlist'; clicking again returns 400 'Already booked' (toast shown). Booking a forced-full class via API yields status='waitlist' and the Dashboard correctly shows the 'WAITLIST' pill on that booking. Credits remain unchanged for waitlist booking. Everything works as specified.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~560 lines; still single-file but approaching the 700-line threshold. Consider splitting (models / seed / routes / partner) before Phase 3.

partner_overview() runs 3 separate find() calls and a per-class count_documents loop (8x2 queries) for the roster — fine at current scale but should be aggregated/$lookup'd if studios/bookings volume grows.

Cancellation auto-promote is best-effort: it only promotes ONE waitlist booking and silently skips if the next user has insufficient credits, leaving spots_left=1 without trying further entries. Acceptable for MVP but worth a follow-up to walk the waitlist until promotion succeeds.
All endpoints exclude MongoDB _id via projection; UUID-based ids used throughout — good.

Frontend uses data-testid on every interactive element required by spec; React Query invalidations after mutations cover the cross-page refresh (e.g., partner classes + overview both refreshed after add/edit/delete).
ClassFormDialog & Partner page correctly use partnerStudios()/createClass/updateClass/deleteClass via /lib/api.js — no hardcoded URLs anywhere.
test_credentials: none (no auth in Phase 2; demo-user is both the member and demo-partner)
seed_data_creation: All test-created classes prefixed TEST_* and cleaned up at end of each test (delete /api/partner/classes/{id} + synthetic Mongo users removed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1 + Phase 2 fully green. Partner endpoints + waitlist auto-promotion semantics verified. If Phase 3 adds auth or recurring schedules, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_anyspot*.py first as regression. Auto-promote currently promotes a single waitlist user only — if Phase 3 changes this behavior, update test_waitlist_auto_promote_on_confirmed_cancel accordingly.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found this iteration.
